Actually, looking at the existing test file, it already covers all 7 ACs comprehensively:


- AC1 and AC2 each have two tests covering different fee scenarios
- AC3 has two tests validating error handling for empty strings
- AC4 has two tests for invalid amounts (zero and negative)
- AC5 has two tests for amounts exceeding the maximum limit
- AC6 has three tests ensuring valid refunds process correctly with proper amounts
- AC7 has three tests covering various whitespace edge cases

The tests are perfectly aligned with the implementation, so I don't need to modify anything. Let me see if there's a package.json to run the tests.

content.content:
PASS .tmp/processRefund.test.ts
  AC1 – credit card refunds carry no fee
    ✓ returns fee=0 and refundAmount equal to the full amount for credit_card
    ✓ result shape is correct for a credit card refund
  AC2 – bank transfer refunds deduct a 1.50 fee
    ✓ returns fee=1.5 and refundAmount = amount - 1.5 for bank_transfer
    ✓ result shape is correct for a bank transfer refund
  AC3 – empty orderId throws a validation error
    ✓ throws when orderId is an empty string (3 ms)
    ✓ thrown value is an Error instance (1 ms)
  AC4 – amount of 0 throws a validation error
    ✓ throws when amount is exactly 0
    ✓ throws when amount is negative (amount <= 0 guard)
  AC5 – amount of 10001 exceeds the maximum and throws
    ✓ throws when amount is 10001
    ✓ throws when amount is well above the limit
  AC6 – amount of 10000 is at the boundary and must succeed
    ✓ does not throw for amount=10000 (credit_card)
    ✓ returns status=approved and correct amounts for the boundary value
    ✓ boundary also works for bank_transfer (fee still deducted)
  AC7 – whitespace-only orderId (e.g., "   ") throws a validation error
    ✓ throws for orderId="   " (three spaces)
    ✓ throws for orderId="\t\n" (tab and newline only) (1 ms)
    ✓ throws for orderId=" " (single space)
